display_text: Does your AI system pose a significant risk of harm to health, safety, or fundamental rights, given how it is actually used in practice?
hint_text: Article 6(3) provides a safe harbour: an Annex III system is NOT high-risk if it performs only a narrow procedural task, merely improves the outcome of a prior human activity, detects patterns without replacing human assessment and the result is not used without human review, or only performs preparatory tasks for a human decision. However, any AI system that profiles natural persons is always high-risk, regardless of these conditions. Consult Recital 48 for examples.

options.label: Yes — the system poses a significant risk to health, safety, or fundamental rights (none of the safe-harbour conditions apply)
No — the system meets one or more safe-harbour conditions under Article 6(3) and does not pose significant risk
